paper_title: Tree-Ring Stable Oxygen Isotopes and Statistical Analysis

methods.method_name: Tree-Ring Stable Oxygen Isotopes Experimental Method
methods.description: This method describes the process of measuring tree-ring widths, extracting α-cellulose, and determining δ18O in α-cellulose to study stable oxygen isotopes in tree rings.

steps.description: The width of each annual ring was measured by the LINTAB measuring table (Rinntech, Germany) with an accuracy of 0.01 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: measure tree-ring widths using LINTAB measuring table

steps.description: The COFECHA program was used for quality control and to ensure accurate calendar years of each ring.
steps.searchable_summary: perform quality control on tree-ring data using COFECHA

steps.description: Developed a ring-width chronology using the ARSTAN program.
Removed age-related signals using negative exponential curve, straight line of negative slope, or horizontal line.
steps.searchable_summary: remove age-related signals from tree-ring data

steps.description: Selected four samples from different trees for stable oxygen isotopes studies.
steps.searchable_summary: select tree-ring samples for isotope analysis

steps.description: Peeled the rings off year by year using a sharp knife and cut the ring samples annually.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare tree-ring samples by peeling and cutting

steps.description: Used the Jayme-Wise method to extract α-cellulose.
Used a Delta V Advantage isotope ratio mass spectrometer to determine the δ18O in α-cellulose.
steps.searchable_summary: determine δ18O using isotope ratio mass spectrometer

steps.description: Inserted a MERCK cellulose every eight samples to calibrate the experimental results.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate δ18O measurements using MERCK cellulose

methods.description: This method involves statistical analysis to explore the response of tree-ring δ18O to climate factors and to reconstruct climate data.

steps.description: Used Pearson's correlation coefficient (r) and partial correlation to explore the response of tree-ring δ18O to climate factors.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Pearson's and partial correlation coefficients

steps.description: Calculated mean interseries correlations (Rbar) and expressed population signal (EPS) based on 30-year windows with a lag of 15 years.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Rbar and EPS for tree-ring data

steps.description: Used statistical parameters to reconstruct the equation, including explained variance (R2), F value, p value, and Durbin-Watson value.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct climate data using statistical parameters

steps.description: Used split calibration-verification model to test the stability of the regression equation.
steps.searchable_summary: validate regression model using split calibration-verification

steps.description: Performed multi-taper method (MTM) spectral analysis to detect periodicities in the reconstructed series.
steps.searchable_summary: perform MTM spectral analysis on reconstructed series

steps.description: Employed ensemble empirical mode decomposition (EEMD) to decompose the reconstruction series.

methods.description: This method describes the collection and processing of meteorological data to support the analysis of tree-ring δ18O data.

steps.description: Downloaded meteorological data from the China Meteorological Service Center.
steps.searchable_summary: download meteorological data from China Meteorological Service Center

steps.description: Obtained δ18Op data from the Global Network of Isotopes in Precipitation project.
Used global grid precipitation data (CRUTS 4.03) to explore spatial representativeness.
steps.searchable_summary: download global grid precipitation data from CRUTS 4.03

steps.description: Calculated correlations between reconstructed series and ENSO-related indices.
